item.title: South Africa's Lekota, who led breakaway from ANC, dies at 77
item.description: South African anti-apartheid activist Mosiuoa Lekota, who broke away from the African National Congress to found a new political party, died in the early hours of Wednesday morning, his party said in a statement. He was 77 years old. Lekota died after a period of illness and had stepped back from

item.title: 173 flights cancelled at HSIA in five days
item.description: A total of 173 international flights have been cancelled at Hazrat Shahjalal International Airport (HSIA) in Dhaka over the past five days as the conflict in the Middle East continues to disrupt regional air travel. Airport authorities said 23 flights were cancelled on Saturday, 40 on Sunday, 46 o

item.title: US-Bangla to run two special flights from Dubai for stranded Bangladeshis
item.description: US-Bangla Airlines will operate two special flights on the Dhaka-Dubai-Dhaka route for Bangladeshis stranded in the United Arab Emirates amid the ongoing war in the Middle East. In a press release issued by the airline on Wednesday, US-Bangla said it is operating the two flights "after taking the

item.title: US envoy Paul Kapur meets Khalilur, discusses bilateral ties
item.description: US Assistant Secretary of State Paul Kapur has met Bangladeshi Minister of Foreign Affairs Khalilur Rahman with the aim of strengthening bilateral relations between the two countries. The meeting took place at the Ministry of Foreign Affairs on Wednesday morning, bdnews24.com reports. Speaking br

item.title: Govt preparing nationwide list of criminals, extortionists to bring them to book: Home Minister
item.description: Home Minister Salahuddin Ahmed today said the government has begun preparing comprehensive lists of criminals and extortionists across the country as part of a decisive drive to restore law and order. “We're preparing the lists of extortionists and criminals in an unbiased way from across th

item.title: Technical, job-oriented education must align with global labour market trends: Education Minister
item.description: Education Minister ANM Ehsanul Hoque Milon on Wednesday underscored the need to modernise and align technical and vocational education with market demands to transform Bangladesh’s vast population into a skilled workforce. “Technical and job-oriented education must be updated in line w

item.title: Festive Red vivo Y31d hits market
item.description: Global smartphone brand vivo has launched its new Y31d in Bangladesh to coincide with the Ramadan season. The smartphone introduces a new and unique color in Bangladesh, Festive Red, and features a 7200mAh BlueVolt battery, the largest ever in vivo’s lineup, aimed at delivering reliable, all-

item.title: Removing non-tariff barriers could boost US investment: Commerce minister
item.description: Commerce, Industry, Textiles and Jute Minister Khandaker Abdul Muktadir has said that eliminating selected non-tariff barriers could significantly boost US investment in Bangladesh, enhancing the country’s appeal as a foreign investment destination. He made the remarks while briefing reporte

item.title: Verdict deferred in Rampura crimes against humanity case as prosecution eyes new evidence
item.description: The verdict in a crimes against humanity case over deaths and injuries in Dhaka’s Rampura during the July 2024 Uprising has been postponed after prosecutors sought time to submit new digital evidence. The International Crimes Tribunal-1, led by Justice Md Golam Mortuza Mozumder, granted the

item.title: 5 Countries that could be a haven
item.description: The drumbeats of global conflict are growing louder, and the phrase 'World War III' is no longer confined to science fiction. As geopolitical tensions simmer between major power blocs, many are asking a chilling question: If world war 3 happens, where can I go to be safe? While no place can ever b

item.title: State minister sits waiting as land office opens late
item.description: State Minister for Land Kayser Kamal had to wait for about half an hour on the veranda of a land office in Narayanganj’s Siddhirganj after arriving for a surprise inspection and finding no officials present. The minister reached the Siddhirganj Union Land Office shortly after 9:00 am on Wedn

item.title: PM reshuffles portfolios of two advisors and eight state ministers
item.description: Prime Minister Tarique Rahman has reassigned the responsibilities of two advisors with the rank of minister and eight state ministers within two weeks of forming his cabinet. The Cabinet Division issued a gazette notification on Wednesday announcing the reshuffle, bdnews24.com reports. According
